title: Button Placement
summary: User-facing placement language for tree, document, context, and scope controls, with Open in VS Code as the first independent placement refactor.
content_html:
Button Placement

Outcome

Docs Viewer puts a control where the user understands its action to be happening. The object of the action chooses the surface; frequency, importance, and desired discoverability choose whether the control is direct or deliberately secondary.

This feature owns that user-facing mental model. The control registry, action targets, controller boundaries, and server authority remain technical concerns owned elsewhere.

Placement Model

User is acting on… Primary placement
Parent/child structure or a subtree Index panel toolbar
The right-clicked row, with deliberately secondary discovery Index panel context menu
Rendered document, source, or display mode Document toolbar
The scope or application as a whole Manage toolbar / Actions

The index context menu acts on the row that was right-clicked, even when another document remains active and displayed. Opening it does not change the active document or the separate checked-document set. This explicit invoked-document target is why the menu can contain unique, rarely used row commands rather than merely duplicating shortcuts for the selected node.

A toolbar control is not justified only by frequency: it may deserve direct placement because it expresses a central operation on that surface.

Whether an action writes data does not choose its placement. Placement follows the user's object of attention; capability projection and the server still decide whether the operation is available and authorized.

Terminology

Term Meaning
action one operation with one behavioural owner
control a visible button, toggle, or menu item that invokes an action
placement the surface on which a control appears
target the document set, invoked document, active document, or scope the action operates on
active document the document currently displayed by the route
invoked document the tree row from which a context-menu command was opened; it need not be the active document
checked documents the separate explicit checkbox set used only by future multi-selection actions
promotion making an existing action directly visible on another appropriate surface without duplicating its behaviour

In user-facing discussion, index toolbar means the toolbar belonging to the active index tree; its technical surface id is index-view. Document toolbar means the toolbar belonging to the active rendered-document view; its technical surface id is main-view. Manage toolbar means the app-level management surface. Actions is a menu within that toolbar, not another name for the entire surface.

Examples

Copy Subtree To Scope

The user is acting on a parent/child set visible in the index tree, so Copy subtree to scope… belongs in the manage-only index toolbar. The active tree document supplies one singular root and the descendants follow automatically. Its cross-scope modal and write workflow do not turn it into a scope-level or document-content control.

Create And Import Endpoints owns the shipped server contract.

Open In VS Code

Open in VS Code is used frequently on the active document's source, so it should be promoted to the document toolbar. The existing context-menu item remains useful because it operates directly on an invoked row without first opening that document.

Both placements must invoke the same action and source-opening behaviour. The context menu supplies the invoked document; the document toolbar supplies the active document. The promotion therefore includes a small target-handoff refactor rather than a second implementation of source opening.

Current Boundary

The placement language is agreed and the shipped Copy Subtree action consumes it. The Open in VS Code promotion remains a separate proposed outcome rather than an extension of the completed copy delivery.

No delivery document is needed until the promotion is scheduled. If implementation remains one small complete slice, this feature can be delivered directly without adding a tracker.

Technical Homes

Toolbar Model and Panel Hosts and Control Surfaces own surface registration, placement projection, and invocation context. Runtime Module Ownership owns the action-definition and focused-controller seam needed to remove the context-menu assumption from source opening.
